spec_match.rationale: The toolbar now uses the current native syntax focusgroup="toolbar inline" instead of the stale standalone horizontal token.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified native arrow-key movement through the toolbar, including skipping the disabled code-style button.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede, support banner, and code sample now describe the behavior-token syntax and explain the v146 listing versus later channel support.

edge_cases.rationale: The demo includes separators, pressed buttons, a disabled button, support detection, and a downstream editor target for Tab exit.

references.rationale: See-also links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5637601087193088, the Open UI explainer, and the Edge demos.

design_system.rationale: Variables drive the toolbar, disabled state, support banner, pressed state, and code sample; no static-card regression.

summary: Re-tested in Chrome Canary through chrome-devtools-mcp at /v146/focusgroup/toolbar-demo/: the page loaded with no console/network errors, the support banner resolved to native, ArrowRight moved from Bold to Align left after three presses, and focus skipped the disabled code-style button. The previous support fallback, disabled-button, and milestone-mismatch questions are resolved.
